name: GCT USB4085-GF-A
designNotes: Mid-mount design sits flush with PCB edge -- reduces overall board thickness
USB 2.0 only -- no SuperSpeed pins connected
Requires 5.1k pull-down resistors on CC1 and CC2 for device mode
Shield pins should connect to ground through 1M resistor + 4.7nF cap for EMI
Popular in custom keyboard designs due to small footprint and mid-mount option
summary: Mid-mount USB-C receptacle — sits flush with PCB edge for minimal thickness
features: Mid-mount design reduces overall board thickness
Through-board tabs provide strong mechanical retention
USB 2.0 with all necessary pins for keyboard use
16-pin full USB-C pinout with shield connections
Rated for 1.5A — sufficient for charging and operation
Popular choice in custom keyboard community
concerns: Mid-mount requires PCB cutout — more complex board layout
Shield connections need EMI filtering components
